pattern: Collapse runs of equal values in a sorted list to a single node by skipping any node equal to its predecessor.
hints: Because the list is sorted, every group of equal values is contiguous, so you only ever compare a node to the one right after it.
Walk a single pointer; whenever the next node holds the same value as the current node, unlink it instead of advancing.
Only advance the current pointer when the next value differs; that keeps exactly the first node of each equal run.

edge_inputs.description: every value identical collapses to one node

stress.description: long sorted list with frequent duplicates
stress.note: small value range over a long list guarantees many duplicate runs
